symbols: God enthroned — divine sovereignty over creation
description: A circular ceiling fresco depicting God the Father (or Creator) enthroned among clouds, surrounded by angels and cherubim. The central figure wears flowing robes in pink/red and green, with a golden halo, making a gesture of benediction. Angels in various colored robes flank Him on either side, some holding instruments or in attitudes of worship. Putti and cherubim fill the lower portion of the composition. The fresco is set within a circular frame decorated with ornate golden scrollwork. The style is characteristic of Perugino's harmonious, idealized Renaissance compositions.
theological_description: This ceiling fresco presents God the Creator enthroned in heavenly glory, surrounded by the angelic court. The circular format (tondo) symbolizes eternity and divine perfection. The image draws on biblical descriptions of God's heavenly throne room from Isaiah 6:1-3, where the prophet sees the Lord seated on a throne with seraphim attending, and from Revelation 4:2-8, where God sits enthroned surrounded by heavenly beings. The gesture of benediction indicates God's ongoing creative and sustaining power over His creation. The angels arranged hierarchically reflect the medieval theological tradition of angelic orders developed from Pseudo-Dionysius.
